industries.name: Jewelry & Decorative Arts
uses: Alloyed at varying karats: 10k (41.7%), 14k (58.3%), 18k (75%), 22k (91.7%) for durability and color
Rings, necklaces, earrings, bracelets, and decorative pendants worldwide
Luxury watch cases, bezels, crowns, and dials (solid gold and plated)
Gold-filled and vermeil pieces bonded or electroplated over base metals
Decorative gilding on furniture, frames, ceramics, and architectural elements

companies.name: Chow Tai Fook Jewellery
companies.use: World's largest jewelry retailer by store count. Sells 999.9-fine pure gold products alongside gem-set jewelry across 7,000+ mainland China stores.

companies.name: LVMH Group
companies.use: Gold is central to Bulgari fine jewelry, Hublot and TAG Heuer watches, and Tiffany & Co. Also used in Louis Vuitton accessories and Dior Joaillerie.

companies.name: Richemont Group
companies.use: Manufactures gold jewelry and watches under Cartier, Van Cleef & Arpels, IWC, Piaget, and Vacheron Constantin.

companies.name: Signet Jewelers
companies.use: North America's largest specialty jewelry retailer, operating Kay Jewelers, Zales, and Jared. Gold rings and chains are the majority of merchandise by value.

companies.name: Pandora
companies.use: Committed to 100% recycled gold. Produces gold charms, rings, and bracelets at scale through its Bangkok crafting facility. 14.5 t confirmed in 2022 sustainability report.

companies.name: Kalyan Jewellers
companies.use: One of India's largest jewelry chains; gold accounts for over 80% of revenue.

companies.name: Luk Fook Holdings
companies.use: Major gold and gem-set jewelry retailer across China, Hong Kong, and Southeast Asia. Specializes in high-purity 999 gold for the Chinese consumer market.

industries.name: Investment & Central Banking
uses: Good Delivery gold bars (400 troy oz, 995+ fine) held as central bank reserves worldwide
Retail investment bars (1 g to 1 kg) and rounds sold to private investors
Legal tender gold coins: American Gold Eagle, Canadian Maple Leaf, South African Krugerrand
Physical gold backing for exchange-traded funds (ETFs) held in secure vaults
Gold allocated accounts representing direct ownership of physical metal

companies.name: SPDR Gold Shares — State Street
companies.use: World's largest gold ETF (GLD). Holds approximately 850–1,000 tonnes of physical gold in HSBC vaults in London, representing billions in investor capital.

companies.name: iShares Gold Trust — BlackRock
companies.use: Second-largest gold ETF (IAU). Holds physical gold in JPMorgan Chase vaults. Lower cost-ratio alternative for retail gold investors globally.

companies.name: Valcambi
companies.use: One of the world's largest gold refineries. Produces the CombiBars — a single bar that can be broken into 50 individual 1-gram units.

companies.name: Perth Mint
companies.use: Government-owned refinery producing gold bars certified to 9999 purity and the Australian Kangaroo gold coin series, sold to investors globally.

companies.name: United States Mint
companies.use: Produces the American Gold Eagle (22-karat) and American Buffalo (24-karat) bullion coins from domestically mined gold.

companies.name: Royal Canadian Mint
companies.use: Produces the world-renowned Maple Leaf gold coin (9999 fine). Known for advanced anti-counterfeiting Bullion DNA technology on coin surfaces.

companies.name: World Gold Council
companies.use: Industry body representing leading gold mining companies. Maintains the LBMA Good Delivery standard and publishes authoritative gold demand statistics.

industries.name: Electronics & Semiconductor Manufacturing
uses: Gold bonding wire (1–50 µm) connecting semiconductor chip die to lead frames
Gold-plated PCB edge connectors and high-cycle switch contacts (corrosion-free)
Gold-tin (80/20) solder for hermetic sealing of high-reliability packages
Gold thick-film pastes and inks for hybrid circuits and LTCC (low-temperature co-fired ceramics) ceramic substrates
Gold-germanium eutectic die-attach for high-power transistors and diodes

companies.name: Heraeus Electronics
companies.use: World's leading supplier of gold bonding wire to the semiconductor industry. Produces thousands of km of gold wire annually, plus gold pastes and thick-film materials.

companies.name: Tanaka Kikinzoku Kogyo
companies.use: Major producer of gold bonding wire, gold-plating solutions, gold paste for electronics, and gold brazing alloys. Supplies foundries, OEMs, and PCB manufacturers.

companies.name: Samsung Electronics
companies.use: Gold wire bonding in DRAM memory modules, NAND flash packages, and mobile processors. Gold-plated contacts in display driver ICs and camera modules.

companies.name: TSMC
companies.use: Uses gold-based metallization layers and bonding wire in advanced node semiconductor fabrication at 3nm–7nm processes. Largest pure-play foundry in the world.

companies.name: Intel Corporation
companies.use: Uses gold bonding wire in all CPU and GPU chip packages. Gold-plated LGA socket contacts on motherboards ensure reliability over thousands of insertion cycles.

companies.name: Texas Instruments
companies.use: Gold wire bonding in high-volume analog, power management, and mixed-signal semiconductor devices. Uses gold-tin solder for precision defense and industrial packaging.

companies.name: Kyocera Corporation
companies.use: Gold-plated connectors and ceramic IC packages for high-reliability electronics (medical, aerospace, industrial). Major user of gold thick-film pastes in LTCC (low-temperature co-fired ceramics) substrates.

industries.name: Dentistry
uses: Type III and IV gold casting alloys for dental crowns, bridges, inlays, and onlays
Gold partial denture frameworks, clasps, and occlusal rests
Gold-titanium hybrid superstructures for implant-supported prosthetics
Gold electroforming for ultra-thin copings bonded to porcelain restorations
Orthodontic brackets and archwires using gold alloys for high strength

companies.name: Metalor Technologies
companies.use: One of the world's leading dental gold producers. Supplies gold alloys to dental labs globally, certified to ISO 22674 for dental metallic materials.

companies.name: Dentsply Sirona
companies.use: World's largest dental products company. Makes gold casting alloys and porcelain-fused-to-metal systems. Gold remains preferred for posterior teeth with high occlusal loads.

companies.name: Degudent (Dentsply subsidiary)
companies.use: Leading supplier of dental gold alloys to European dental laboratories. Produces type III and IV gold casting alloys for both traditional casting and CAD/CAM disc milling.

companies.name: Ivoclar Vivadent
companies.use: Produces dental gold alloys and IPS systems that bond ceramic to gold frameworks. Gold-based materials remain preferred for posterior teeth with high occlusal loads.

companies.name: GC Corporation
companies.use: Major supplier of dental gold alloys for Asian markets. Manufactures casting alloys compatible with both lost-wax casting and modern pressable ceramic systems.

companies.name: Coltene Group
companies.use: Dental materials company producing gold-based investment casting alloys and dental lab materials. Serves dental laboratories and clinics in over 100 countries.

industries.name: Aerospace & Defense
uses: Gold-coated polyimide (Kapton) thermal blankets on satellites (reflects 98% of infrared)
Gold-plated MIL-SPEC electrical connectors for corrosion-free reliability over decades
Gold coating on astronaut visor outer layer — filters UV and infrared radiation
Gold-tin brazing alloys in jet engine heat exchangers and precision assemblies
Gold-coated mirrors in infrared sensors, LIDAR systems, and space telescopes

companies.name: NASA
companies.use: Gold-coated all 18 beryllium mirror segments of the James Webb Space Telescope. Extensive use of gold Kapton thermal blankets on spacecraft. Astronaut helmet visors are gold-coated.

companies.name: Lockheed Martin
companies.use: Gold thermal insulation on Orion crew capsule and classified defense satellites. Gold-plated connectors throughout F-35 avionics; gold in infrared sensor arrays.

companies.name: Boeing
companies.use: Gold-coated thermal control blankets on Boeing commercial satellites (702 platform). Gold electrical contacts in 787 Dreamliner and 777X advanced avionics systems.

companies.name: Raytheon Technologies
companies.use: Gold-plated contacts in Patriot missile guidance electronics and radar systems. Gold in infrared focal plane arrays used in Stinger and Javelin missile seekers.

companies.name: Airbus Defence & Space
companies.use: Gold multi-layer insulation on Airbus communications satellites. Gold brazing alloys in Ariane launch vehicle propulsion components.

companies.name: Thales Group
companies.use: Gold-plated connectors and gold wire bonding in radar, sonar, and avionics systems. Gold coatings on infrared detector arrays for Rafale fighter jet targeting pods.

industries.name: Medical Devices & Pharmaceuticals
uses: Radiopaque gold markers (4–6 per stent) for X-ray guidance during coronary artery placement
Gold electrodes in cochlear implants, pacemakers, and deep brain stimulators
Gold sodium thiomalate (Myocrisin) for rheumatoid arthritis treatment
Gold nanoparticles in rapid lateral flow diagnostic tests (pregnancy, COVID-19, HIV)
Gold nanoparticle photothermal cancer therapy — targeted heating of tumor cells

companies.name: Boston Scientific
companies.use: Uses gold radiopaque markers on the SYNERGY and PROMUS coronary stent families. Gold markers enable cardiologists to precisely position stents under fluoroscopy guidance.

companies.name: Medtronic
companies.use: Gold electrodes in EnRhythm pacemakers, Activa deep brain stimulators, and cochlear implants. Gold ensures long-term biocompatibility and stable electrical properties.

companies.name: Abbott Laboratories
companies.use: Gold markers on Xience coronary stent platform. Uses gold nanoparticles in i-STAT rapid diagnostic cartridges and lateral flow immunoassay products.

companies.name: Stryker
companies.use: Gold-marked neuro and spinal implants for imaging visibility. Gold in neurovascular coils used to treat brain aneurysms.

companies.name: Cochlear Limited
companies.use: Gold-plated electrode arrays (22 electrodes) in cochlear implants placed inside the inner ear. Gold ensures electrical precision and corrosion resistance over decades.

companies.name: Nanospectra Biosciences
companies.use: Pioneered gold nanoshell photothermal therapy for prostate cancer. Gold nanoparticles accumulate in tumors and convert near-infrared light to heat, destroying cancer cells.

industries.name: Chemical Catalysis
uses: Supported gold nanoparticle catalysts for CO oxidation at room temperature
Gold-palladium bimetallic catalysts for vinyl acetate monomer (VAM) production
Pd-Au alloy catalysts for hydrogen peroxide synthesis (greener hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂) production)
Gold catalysts for selective hydrogenation in pharmaceutical intermediate synthesis
Gold-silver alloy catalysts for formaldehyde and methanol partial oxidation

companies.name: Johnson Matthey
companies.use: Leading commercial producer of gold-palladium catalysts for vinyl acetate monomer (VAM) production. Supplies gold CO-oxidation catalysts for industrial safety and emissions control.

companies.name: Heraeus
companies.use: Produces gold catalyst materials and supports research in gold catalysis through its Precious Metals Division. Supplies gold-based materials to chemical process industries worldwide.

companies.name: Umicore
companies.use: Precious metal catalyst specialist producing gold-based catalysts for industrial chemical processes. Also a major gold refiner and recycler.

companies.name: BASF SE
companies.use: World's largest chemical company; uses gold-based catalysts in specialty chemical manufacturing. Active research in gold nanocatalyst applications for selective oxidation.

companies.name: Evonik Industries
companies.use: Uses gold-palladium (Pd-Au) alloy catalysts in its HPPO process for hydrogen peroxide production — a greener synthesis route used in bleaching and chemical manufacturing.

companies.name: World Gold Council (Catalyst R&D)
companies.use: Coordinates international research into new gold catalyst applications through partnerships with universities and industry. Publishes the definitive database of gold-catalyzed reactions.
